hotels.name: Freehand Miami Hostel
hotels.address: 2727 Indian Creek Drive, Miami Beach, FL 33140

activities.name: South Beach Exploration
activities.description: Walk along the famous South Beach, enjoy the Art Deco architecture, and relax on the sandy shores. Visit Ocean Drive and Lincoln Road Mall.

activities.name: Dinner at La Sandwicherie
activities.description: Casual French sandwich shop known for fresh ingredients and affordable prices. A Miami Beach institution since 1988.

activities.name: Wynwood Walls Street Art Tour
activities.description: Explore the vibrant Wynwood Walls outdoor museum featuring colorful murals and street art from world-renowned artists. Free to visit.

activities.name: Lunch at Coyo Taco
activities.description: Authentic Mexican street tacos in the heart of Wynwood. Great prices and atmosphere.

activities.name: Vizcaya Museum and Gardens
activities.description: Explore this stunning Italian Renaissance-style villa with beautiful gardens overlooking Biscayne Bay. A National Historic Landmark.

activities.name: Dinner at Versailles Restaurant
activities.description: Iconic Cuban restaurant in Little Havana. Experience authentic Cuban cuisine at reasonable prices.

activities.name: Little Havana Walking Tour
activities.description: Stroll down Calle Ocho, visit Domino Park, see cigar rollers at work, and experience the vibrant Cuban culture. Self-guided tour.

activities.name: Lunch at El Rey de las Fritas
activities.description: Grab a quick and delicious Cuban frita burger before heading to the airport.

plan.reasoning: This weekend trip to Miami fits well within the $900 budget at $586 total. I selected Spirit Airlines for budget-friendly nonstop flights from Chicago O'Hare to Miami ($300 roundtrip), which is typical for this route. For accommodation, I chose Freehand Miami, a popular hostel-style property with private rooms that offers great value at $89/night ($178 for 2 nights) while still providing good amenities and a prime Miami Beach location. The itinerary includes a mix of free activities (South Beach, Wynwood Walls, Little Havana) and one paid attraction (Vizcaya Museum at $25), along with budget-friendly dining options at local favorites. The schedule allows for arrival on Friday morning with beach time, a full day of exploration on Saturday, and a relaxed Sunday morning before an evening flight home. Total spending is $586, leaving $314 under budget for transportation, incidentals, and additional meals.
plan.final_message_to_user:
I've planned your weekend getaway to Miami! Your trip comes to $586, well under your $900 budget.

**Flight Summary:** Spirit Airlines nonstop flights - departing Chicago Friday morning (6:15 AM) and returning Sunday evening (8:30 PM) for $300 total.

**Hotel:** Freehand Miami Hostel in Miami Beach - a trendy, budget-friendly option with a private room for $89/night ($178 total). It has a pool, bar, and is close to the beach.

**Highlights:**
- Friday: Arrive and explore iconic South Beach and Ocean Drive
- Saturday: Visit the colorful Wynwood Walls street art, tour the beautiful Vizcaya Museum & Gardens, and experience authentic Cuban cuisine in Little Havana
- Sunday: Morning stroll through Little Havana before your evening flight

**Meals included:** $83 for dining at local favorites like La Sandwicherie, Coyo Taco, and the famous Versailles Cuban restaurant.

You'll have $314 remaining for Ubers/Lyfts, additional meals, drinks, and souvenirs. Have a great trip!
